w:r.w:t: Design and Implementation of an AI Web-Based Cooperative Savings and Loan Management Information System for Al-Halal Cooperative Multipurpose Society, FUT Minna
Proposal
BY
USMAN Alhassan
Admission 2510801300122
Programme: B. Sc Computer and Information Technology
JUNE, 2026
1.0 Introduction
1.1 Background to the Study
The rapid advancement of information and communication technologies has transformed the way organizations manage information, deliver services, and interact with

stakeholders. Modern organizations increasingly rely on web-based information systems to automate operations, improve efficiency, and enhance service delivery. Cooperatives, which play a significant role in promoting savings, credit access, and economic em
powerment among members, are also experiencing the need for digital transformation to remain efficient and competitive (Dawson et al., 2024; Zheng et al., 2024).
Despite the benefits associated with digital technologies, many cooperative societies in Niger

ia still depend on manual record-keeping systems for managing member registrations, savings, loans, shares, and financial transactions. These manual methods are often characterized by delayed information retrieval, data redundancy, calculation errors, poor
record security, and difficulties in generating reports for
annual report and
decision-making purposes (Miranti et al., 2024).

Al-Halal Cooperative Multipurpose Society (ACMS) is a registered cooperative organization established by concerned individuals who voluntarily came together to promote mutual economic benefits and harmonious relationships based on Islamic principles. The society was founded primarily to serve the staff of the Federal University of Technology (FUT), Minna, Niger State, Nigeria. Guided by the Islamic principle that
"Allah has permitted trading and forbidden usury (Riba),"
the cooperative operates in accordance with Shariah-compliant financial practices, ensuring that all its activities are free from interest-based transactions.

The formal operations of the society commenced in 2003 with an initial membership of approximately 27 members. Since its establishment, the cooperative has continued to grow in membership and financial activities, providing various services such as savings, interest-free loans, investment opportunities, and other welfare schemes aimed at improving the economic well-being of its members.

The society is governed by an Executive Committee (EXCO), supported by several standing committees, including the Shurah Committee, Business Committee, Credit Committee, and other ad hoc committees constituted whenever necessary. These committees are responsible for policy
formulation, loan administration, investment decisions, and the overall management of the cooperative's affairs.

Despite its growth, Al-Halal Cooperative Multipurpose Society currently relies largely on manual methods for managing its daily operations. Member registration, savings contributions, loan applications, loan repayments, shareholdings, and financial records are maintained using paper-based files and ledger books. This manual approach results in time-consuming processes, difficulties in retrieving records, increased risk of data loss or errors, and delays in generating financial reports. Members also experience delays in accessing up-to-date information regarding their savings balances, loan status, and transaction history.

Advancements in information technology have demonstrated that web-based Cooperative Management Information Systems significantly improve the efficiency, transparency, accountability, and reliability of cooperative operations through centralized data management, automated transaction processing, secure record keeping, and real-time information access. Consequently, there is a need to design and implement a web-based Cooperative Management Information System for Al-Halal Cooperative Multipurpose Society to automate its operations, improve administrative efficiency, enhance service delivery, and provide members with timely access to accurate financial information.
1.1 Statement of the Problem
The current manual system employed by
Alhalal Cooperative Multipurpose Society
presents several operational challenges. These include difficulties in maintaining accurate member records, delays in process
ing savings and loan transactions, challenges in generating financial reports, increased risk of data loss, and limited access to information by members.
Furthermore, members must physically visit the cooperative office to obtain information regarding thei
r savings balances, loan repayments, and account statements
and also apply manually
. This process is time-consuming and may negatively affect member satisfaction. The absence of a computerized management system limits operational efficiency, transparency,

and effective decision-making within the cooperative. Consequently, there is a need for a web-based solution capable of automating cooperative activities and providing efficient information management.
Aim of the Study
The aim of this study is to design and implement a Web-Based Cooperative Management Information System for
Alhalal Cooperative Multipurpose Society
The specific objectives are to:
Examine the existing management processes of
Alhalal Cooperative Multipurpose Society
Design a web-based database system for managing cooperative records and transactions.
Develop modules for member registration, savings management, loan management, and share management.
Implement a member portal for viewing account information
and transaction history.
Develop reporting functionalities for generating financial reports and account statements.
Evaluate the performance and usability of the developed system.
Also make it user friendly for members to apply for loan online
Significance of the Study
The study will benefit
Alhalal Cooperative Multipurpose Society
by providing a computerized platform for managing cooperative activities more efficiently. The system will reduce paperwork, improve data accuracy, facilitate report
generation, and enhance transparency in financial transactions.
The study will also benefit cooperative members by providing faster access to account information, savings balances, loan status, and transaction records. Furthermore, the study will contribu
te to existing literature on cooperative information systems and digital transformation within cooperative organizations.
Scope of the Study
The proposed system will focus on:
Member registration and profile management.
Savings management.
Loan
management and repayment tracking.
Share management.
Transaction processing.
Report generation.
User authentication and access control.
The study is limited to
Alhalal Cooperative Multipurpose Society
Methodology
The study will adopt the Agile Softwar
e Development Methodology due to its iterative and flexible approach to system development. Data will be collected through interviews, observation, and examination of existing cooperative records.
The system will be developed using:
Frontend: React.js and
Tailwind CSS
Backend: Django REST Framework
Database: PostgreSQL
Reporting Tool: ReportLab PDF Generator
The developed system will be tested and evaluated based on functionality, usability, reliability, and user satisfaction.
Expected Outcome

The study is expected to produce a functional web-based Cooperative Management Information System capable of automating member registration, savings management, loan administration, share management, transaction processing, and report generation. The syste
m is also expected to improve operational efficiency, transparency, and member satisfaction within Alhal Multipurpose Cooperative Society.
References

Abdulkadir, S., Hussaini, A., Ango, Y. I., & Muhammad, R. A. (2024). Development and implementation of an integrated food commodities reservation and management system for the KASU Majlis cooperative society using structured approach.
Science World Journal, 19
Dawson, G. E., Antunes Jr., J. A. V., Wegner, D., & Adami, V. S. (2024). Creating a digital platform for the agricultural cooperative system through interorganizational collaboration.
Journal of Rural Studies, 110
Miranti, A. T., Fatoni, F., Andri, A., & Erlansyah, D. (2024). Utilizing web engineering to build a savings and loans information system in a Plasma Pratama Mandiri Cooperative.
Journal of Information Systems and Informatics, 6
(2). https://doi.org/10.51519/journalisi.v6i2.723
Ratnawati, F., & Santoso, B. (2024). Development of a digital management system in cooperatives for operational efficiency improvement.
INOVTEK Polbeng - Seri Informatika, 9
Zheng, Y., Mei, L., & Chen, W. (2024). Does government policy matter in the digital transformation of farmers' cooperatives? A tripartite evolutionary game analysis.
Frontiers in Sustainable Food Systems, 8
